title: Fired Brick & Gypsum
subtitle: Sana'a highland tower house tradition
content: Standards for fired brick construction with gypsum render — the defining material system of Sana'a Old City and the highland tower house tradition. Covers brick specification, gypsum banding (diwan friezes), qamaria window fabrication, and minaret construction following Marchand's documentation.
specs: Local fired clay · 22 × 10 × 6 cm · min 15 N/mm² compressive strength
Diwan frieze bands at every floor level · min 150mm height · white gypsum only
Alabaster or coloured glass · geometric or floral pattern · YAI catalogue Appendix Q
Gypsum-lime mortar (1:1) for heritage · Portland cement strictly prohibited on exterior
30–40% of facade area maximum · small openings north face · qamaria south/east
Per Marchand (2001) — gypsum-bonded brick courses 55–65mm · tapered 1:20
traditional: Traditional Sana'a — local fired brick, white gypsum diwan bands, stained-glass qamaria fanlights, no modern finishes.
transitional: Transitional — fired brick structure with cement core permitted if exterior face is traditional brick and gypsum render.
contemporary: Contemporary highland — brick or concrete structure with fired brick cladding minimum 90mm + gypsum render on all street-facing facades.
